perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, the Company hereby appoints the Agent as a placement agent, on a non-exclusive basis, to utilize its "best efforts" to solicit subscriptions for the Shares and to assist the Company with respect to the Company's sale of the Shares in the Offering. On the basis of the representations and warranties and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Agent accepts such appointment. The Agent shall not be obligated to purchase any Shares and shall not be obligated to take any action which Agent deems to be inconsistent with any applicable law, regulation, decision or order. Subscriptions will be offered as described in the Registration Statement. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, the appointment of the Agent will terminate upon completion, expiration or termination of the Offering.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, the Company hereby appoints the Agent as a representative, on a non-exclusive basis, to utilize its "reasonable efforts" to solicit subscriptions for the Shares and to assist the Company with respect to the Company's sale of the Shares in the Offering. On the basis of the representations and warranties and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Agent accepts such appointment. The Agent shall not be obligated to purchase any Shares and shall not be obligated to take any action which Agent deems to be inconsistent with any applicable law, regulation, decision or order. Subscriptions will be offered as described in the Registration Statement. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, the appointment of the Agent will terminate upon completion, expiration or termination of the Offering.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'placement agent' is replaced with 'representative' which is not defined, and the term 'best efforts' is changed to 'reasonable efforts'. This creates ambiguity as the contract later refers back to the Agent's initial role using 'Agent' but doesn't clarify if it is the 'representative' or the 'placement agent' is the same. The switch from 'best efforts' to 'reasonable efforts' introduces a significant legal difference which impacts the Agent's required exertion levels, thus changing the obligation and creating uncertainty about the scope of the Agent's responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company represents and warrants to the Agent that: a. The Company has all such power, authority, authorizations, approvals and orders as may be required to enter into this Agreement, to carry out the provisions and conditions hereof and to issue and sell the Shares as contemplated herein and as described in the Registration Statement, any Preliminary Prospectuses, the Prospectus or any Permitted Free Writing Prospectus. The consummation of the Offering, the execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement and the consummation of the transactions contemplated herein have been duly and validly authorized by all necessary corporate action on the part of the Company and this Agreement has been validly executed and delivered by the Company and is the valid, legal and binding agreement of the Company enforceable in accordance with its terms, except to the extent, if any, that the provisions of Section 10 hereof may be unenforceable as against public policy, and except to the extent that such enforceability may be limited by bankruptcy laws, insolvency laws, or other laws affecting the enforcement of creditors' rights generally.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'placement agent' to 'representative' and 'best efforts' to 'reasonable efforts' introduces ambiguity and a significant legal difference regarding the Agent's responsibilities. The contradicted text, which includes representations and warranties made to the 'Agent', now lacks clarity because the term 'Agent' is no longer precisely defined. This uncertainty undermines the enforceability of the representations and warranties, as it's unclear to which entity ('placement agent' or 'representative') the Company made those assurances. The switch from 'best efforts' to 'reasonable efforts' further exacerbates the contradiction by altering the Agent's required level of exertion, potentially making the original representations about the Agent's responsibilities inaccurate or unenforceable. This falls under the definition of misaligned terminology, causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Agent shall receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: a. The fee shall be equal to 5.2% of the "gross proceeds" received in the Offering attributable to the efforts of the Agent. At least 48 hours prior to each Closing, the Agent shall deliver a schedule, identified as Appendix A, that lists each investor and the amount of investment.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agent shall receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: a. The commission shall be equal to 5.2% of the "gross proceeds" received in the Offering attributable to the efforts of the Agent. At least 48 hours prior to each Closing, the Agent shall deliver a schedule, identified as Appendix A, that lists each investor and the amount of investment.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'fee' is replaced with 'commission.' While both refer to compensation, the legal implications can differ (e.g., in bankruptcy proceedings or tax treatment). The contract now inconsistently uses both terms, leading to potential disputes over the exact nature of the payment and how it should be legally classified. This introduces an in-text contradiction due to unclear interchangeable use of the terms commission and fee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company shall direct the Escrow Agent to deliver payment of the fees due to the Agent pursuant to Section 2 hereof (less any portion thereof previously paid to the Agent) to the Agent by wire transfer or certified or bank cashier's check drawn to the order of the Agent in next day funds, to the Agent on each Closing Date.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'fee' with 'commission.' The contradicted text refers to 'fees due to the Agent'. If 'fee' and 'commission' are not explicitly defined as interchangeable, the change creates an ambiguity regarding whether the contradicted text now also includes 'commissions' or only refers to 'fees'. This discrepancy could lead to disputes over payment obligations. Therefore, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Agent will not provide any service or engage in any activity, and it will not permit the Agent or any of its employees, agents, representatives or affiliates to provide any service or engage in any activity, whether pursuant to this Agreement or otherwise in connection with the Offering, for which it or he does not have in effect all registrations, licenses and approvals necessary to cause that service or activity to comply with applicable federal and state laws and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: The Sales Personnel will not provide any service or engage in any activity, and it will not permit the Sales Personnel or any of its employees, agents, representatives or affiliates to provide any service or engage in any activity, whether pursuant to this Agreement or otherwise in connection with the Offering, for which it or he does not have in effect all registrations, licenses and approvals necessary to cause that service or activity to comply with applicable federal and state laws and regulations.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Agent' to 'Sales Personnel' without defining 'Sales Personnel' and without consistently applying this new term throughout the contract creates confusion. The agent is defined, however the "Sales Personnel" is not, as a result, it leads to confusion on if they are reffering to the Agent or Sales Personnel. The other sections still reference “Agent”, making it ambiguous who bears the responsibility for compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Agent, and its officers, directors, agents, representatives and affiliates and any other person, if any, who controls the Agent or its affiliates within the meaning of the 1933 Act (these parties together with the Agent are hereinafter referred to as the "Agent Indemnitees") against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities and expenses (including reasonable costs of investigation and counsel's fees) arising out of or based upon the actions of the Company or any of its employees, agents, representatives or affiliates (i) that constitute bad faith or gross negligence on the part of the Company or its employees, agents, representatives or affiliates, (ii) that constitute violations of applicable federal or state laws or regulations on the part of the Company or its employees, agents, representatives or affiliates, or (iii) that constitute a violation of any of the Company's agreements, representations or warranties contained in this Agreement. The Company will reimburse the Agent and the Agent Indemnitees for any legal or other expenses reasonably incurred (individually or collectively) by it or them in connection with investigating or defending any such loss, claim, damage, liability or action. However, the Company will not be responsible for (i) any losses, claims, damages, liabilities or expenses that result from bad faith or gross negligence on the part of the Agent or any of its employees, agents, representatives or affiliates, or on the part of the Agent Indemnitee, (ii) that arise out of actions or conduct by the Agent or any of its employees, agents, representatives or affiliates, or by any Agent Indemnitee, that constitute a violation of any applicable federal or state law or regulation, or (iii) that arise out of actions or conduct by the Agent or any of its employees, agents, representatives or affiliates, or by any Agent Indemnitee, that constitutes a violation of any of Agent's agreements, representations or warranties contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the 'Agent' who is defined, and the contradicted text also refers to the 'Agent'. The changed text replaces 'Agent' with 'Sales Personnel', which is undefined within the contract. This misalignment in terminology creates a direct conflict because the indemnity clause specifically protects the 'Agent', and it is now unclear if 'Sales Personnel' are also indemnified, leading to ambiguity about who bears the responsibility and liability. The change directly contradicts the contradicted text, making it unenforceable to the degree to which it is meant to cover 'Sales Personnel'.
